source_jobs.title: Senior ML Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Adelphi: Adelphi builds AI/ML-enabled secure data access and sharing technology for the U.S. intelligence and defense communities, using its Connector software product to enable federated data discovery that cuts intelligence-sharing time from months to minutes. The company's mission is to eliminate data silos, build trust in automation without compromising security, and improve information flow across mission-critical environments. Adelphi closed a $7M Seed round in August 2025 and has Customers across the Intelligence Community and the Department of War.

About the Role: The Forward Deployed Senior Machine Learning Engineer position requires a mix of software development, LLM Ops, and secdevops practices, resulting in an exciting, fast-paced engineering role. This role requires the ability to provide solutions for the full LLM stack, from the OS, storage, and network up to the API and transport layer. Experience in the defense or intelligence fields is required. You will own end-to-end delivery of agentic, full-stack systems built on top of frontier models, from first prototype to stable production, embedded alongside our defense and intelligence customers.

Location: This role is based in the DC/Metro area; remote candidates will be considered with 25% travel expected.

Clearance Requirement: An active U.S. Government clearance is strongly preferred, but we are open to clearance eligible candidates.

Is this you?

We are an AI-native engineering team. We expect every engineer to leverage LLMs and AI tooling as a core part of how they design, build, ship, and operate agentic systems that turn frontier-model capability into mission outcomes.

 - You use AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot) daily and instinctively.

 - You prompt-engineer through complex architecture and debug sessions, leverage LLMs across the full development lifecycle, maintain a clear-eyed view of AI limitations in high-security contexts, and stay current with emerging models and tooling.

 - Hands-on with modern agent frameworks and SDKs (LangGraph, OpenAI Agents SDK, Claude Agent SDK, AutoGen, or similar) and agent evaluation / observability tooling.

 - Familiarity with MCP or similar LLM integration frameworks.

 - You have a clear-eyed view of AI limitations. You know when to trust AI-generated output and when to verify.

Expectations:

 - Own end-to-end delivery of agentic, full-stack systems from first prototype to stable production, embedded alongside defense and intelligence customers.

 - Build and deploy ML services leveraging LLMs, embeddings, RAG, and agent orchestration into production environments, including classified and air-gapped ones.

 - Work directly with customers to scope problems, sequence delivery, and ship novel AI applications under real-world constraints.

 - Codify repeatable patterns into reusable tools and building blocks that help the team ship faster.

Bonus Points:

 - Proficiency in infrastructure management (Docker, Kubernetes, AWS).

 - Expertise in encryption, authentication, Linux systems administration, DevOps, or SRE.

 - Production experience launching agentic services and forward-deployed AI applications that drive operational value at the customer site.

 - Experience as a forward-deployed engineer or tech-led delivery role, embedded with mission customers, scoping problems, sequencing delivery, and shipping novel agentic applications under tight constraints

 - Experience with federated or privacy-preserving data architectures (such as differential privacy and secure enclaves).

Benefits:

 - Healthcare coverage: 100% employee premium and 50% dependents premium coverage of a platinum-level plan.

 - 401K with 2% company match.

 - $500 monthly Physical and Mental Health reimbursement program.

 - Unlimited time-off policy.

 - Competitive salary and equity compensation.

 - Opportunity to work on impactful projects in the national security sector.

 - Career growth and leadership opportunities i

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Adelphi: Adelphi builds AI/ML-enabled secure data access and sharing technology for the U.S. intelligence and defense communities, using its Connector software product to enable federated data discovery that cuts intelligence-sharing time from months to minutes. The company's mission is to eliminate data silos, build trust in automation without compromising security, and improve information flow across mission-critical environments. Adelphi closed a $7M Seed round in August 2025 and has Customers across the Intelligence Community and the Department of War.

About the Role: As Senior Software Engineer, you will be making technical direction and architectural decisions for the platform. This role requires a mix of software development and ML Ops practices. The right candidate will bring deep experience building, shipping, and supporting mission-critical services across the full data stack. Experience in the national security field is a strong plus to best understand Customer requirements and how that will drive the product roadmap. We are an AI-native engineering team. We expect every engineer to leverage LLMs and AI tooling as a core part of how they design, build, ship, and debug software as an integral, daily extension of their workflow.

Clearance Requirement: An active U.S. Government Secret clearance is not required. Candidates must be U.S. citizens and be eligible to maintain and upgrade clearances as the role requires. Candidates need to be excited to work for the Defense and Intel Communities, and to apply AI to real applications for Government.

Is this you?

We are an AI-native engineering team. We expect every engineer to leverage LLMs and AI tooling as a core part of how they design, build, ship, and operate agentic systems that turn frontier-model capability into mission outcomes.

 - Technical Leadership: You have 8+ years of experience with meaningful time at Senior level or above. You own architecture for mission-critical production systems, think in systems and trade-offs, and thrive in ambiguous environments with high ownership.

 - Domain & Tech Stack: You have experience handling and securing large-scale production data, are proficient with AWS and Python (Flask, FastAPI, or Django), and are comfortable in TypeScript/JavaScript (React, Next.js). Experience in government or national security environments is a strong plus.

 - AI-Native by Default: You use AI coding tools daily and instinctively, prompt-engineer through complex architecture and debug sessions, and maintain a clear-eyed view of AI limitations in high-security contexts. You know when to trust AI-generated output and when to verify.

 - Mission-Driven: You are excited by how AI enables better solutions to hard, long-standing problems like data sharing, and you want to see that work deployed in environments where it matters.
   

Bonus Points:

 - Vector store deployment at enterprise scale.

 - Infrastructure management (Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, GCP, On-Premise).

 - Encryption, authentication, DevOps, or SRE expertise.

 - Production experience launching agentic services leveraging LLMs, embeddings, and inference.

Benefits:

 - Healthcare coverage: 100% employee premium and 50% dependents premium coverage of a platinum-level plan.

 - 401K with 2% company match.

 - $500 monthly Physical and Mental Health reimbursement program.

 - Unlimited time-off policy.

 - Competitive salary and equity compensation.

 - Opportunity to work on impactful projects in the national security sector.

 - Career growth and leadership opportunities in a dynamic, innovative environment.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Adelphi: Adelphi builds AI/ML-enabled secure data access and sharing technology for the U.S. intelligence and defense communities, using its Connector software product to enable federated data discovery that cuts intelligence-sharing time from months to minutes. The company's mission is to eliminate data silos, build trust in automation without compromising security, and improve information flow across mission-critical environments. Adelphi closed a $7M Seed round in August 2025 and has Customers across the Intelligence Community and the Department of War.

About the Role: As a Software Engineer, you will contribute to technical direction and help build and maintain mission-critical platform features. This role requires a mix of software development and ML Ops practices. The right candidate will bring solid experience building and shipping production services across the full data stack. Experience in the national security field is a plus. We are an AI-native engineering team. We expect every engineer to leverage LLMs and AI tooling as a core part of how they design, build, ship, and debug software as an integral, daily extension of their workflow.

Location: This role is based in the DC/Metro area; remote candidates will be considered with 20% travel expected.

Clearance Requirement: An active U.S. Government clearance is strongly preferred, but we are open to clearance eligible candidates.

Is this you?

We are an AI-native engineering team. We expect every engineer to leverage LLMs and AI tooling as a core part of how they design, build, ship, and operate agentic systems that turn frontier-model capability into mission outcomes.

 - Experience: You have 4-7 years of software engineering experience and are comfortable owning features end-to-end in a production environment. You think in systems, ask good questions, and thrive with autonomy.

 - Domain & Tech Stack: You have experience working with production data, are proficient in Python (Flask, FastAPI, or Django), and are comfortable in TypeScript/JavaScript (React, Next.js). Familiarity with AWS is a plus. Interest in government or national security environments is a strong plus.

 - AI-Native by Default: You use AI coding tools daily and instinctively, leverage LLMs across your development workflow, and have a clear-eyed view of when to trust AI-generated output and when to verify.

 - Mission-Driven: You are excited by how AI enables better solutions to hard, long-standing problems like data sharing, and you want to see that work deployed in environments where it matters.
   

Bonus Points:

 - Familiarity with vector stores or embedding-based systems.

 - Infrastructure experience (Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, GCP).

 - Exposure to encryption, authentication, DevOps, or SRE practices.

 - Any production experience with agentic services leveraging LLMs, embeddings, or inference.

Benefits:

 - Healthcare coverage: 100% employee premium and 50% dependents premium coverage of a platinum-level plan.

 - 401K with 2% company match.

 - $500 monthly Physical and Mental Health reimbursement program.

 - Unlimited time-off policy.

 - Competitive salary and equity compensation.

 - Opportunity to work on impactful projects in the national security sector.

 - Career growth and leadership opportunities in a dynamic, innovative environment.

source_jobs.title: Information Systems Security Manager (ISSM)
source_jobs.description:
About Adelphi: Adelphi builds AI/ML-enabled secure data access and sharing technology for the U.S. intelligence and defense communities, using its Connector software product to enable federated data discovery that cuts intelligence-sharing time from months to minutes. The company's mission is to eliminate data silos, build trust in automation without compromising security, and improve information flow across mission-critical environments. Adelphi closed a $7M Seed round in August 2025 and has Customers across the Intelligence Community and the Department of War.

About the Role: As Adelphi’s first ISSM, you will own the security program for Adelphi. You will drive Adelphi’s work through Dept of War authorization on the IL2 (NIPR) path and onto a defensible IL5 trajectory, operating on a continuous-ATO cadence that matches our weekly sprint pace. This role sits at the intersection of security architecture, compliance program management, and day-to-day security operations. You will be the primary interface to the Dept of War Authorizing Official, assessors, and our customer program office, while working shoulder-to-shoulder with a small, high-velocity engineering team.

Clearance Requirement: Active Top Secret/SCI

Is this you?

 - Security Program Ownership: You've owned a government security program end-to-end, from initial authorization through continuous monitoring, and know how to keep it moving inside a fast-paced engineering team without becoming a bottleneck. You have personally led at least one DoD or IC system through the NIST RMF authorization process to an ATO and sustained it through continuous monitoring. Prior ISSM or ISSO experience on a DoD or IC contract is required; experience as a Prime contractor is strongly preferred.

 - Experience: 8+ years in cybersecurity, information assurance, or security engineering, with a substantial portion directly supporting DoD or IC systems. You have a track record of delivering this work reliably and under scrutiny.

 - Security Architecture & Technical Depth: You think at the architecture level across identity, authorization, cryptography, and audit. You've worked in environments where the security boundaries between systems matter, and you know how to design for them deliberately. Experience with FedRAMP inheritance modeling and CSP shared-responsibility mapping is a plus.

 - Agentic AI & Emerging Tech: You're familiar with how AI agents interact with data and tools, and you've thought seriously about what securing those interactions looks like, even if the specific stack is new to you. Familiarity with Palantir Foundry, O365/SharePoint, or Databricks in classified or IL5 environments is a bonus.

 - Security Operations & Incident Response: You've run security operations and led incident response in complex environments. You write clear, structured findings for both technical and executive audiences.

 - Stakeholder Management: You've been the primary security interface to government customers, assessors, and internal engineering leadership, and you know how to manage all of them at once.

 - Certifications: A current DoD 8140 / 8570 IAM-qualifying certification is required at hire. CISSP or CISM (IAM Level III) strongly preferred; CASP+ accepted. You must be able to maintain certification currency throughout the role.

 - Bonus points if you have prior experience standing up a security program at a startup or small company

Benefits:

 - Healthcare coverage: 100% employee premium and 50% dependents premium coverage of a platinum-level plan.

 - 401K with 2% company match.

 - $500 monthly Physical and Mental Health reimbursement program.

 - Unlimited time-off policy.

 - Competitive salary and equity compensation.

 - Opportunity to work on impactful projects in the national security sector.

 - Career growth and leadership opportunities in a dynamic, innovative environment.

notes: Defense-focused AI/ML startup with strong engineering core
